name: Eleven Madison Park
description: Fine-dining reference — bone-cream and museum-green canvas with editorial serif at hero scale, ceremonial whitespace, near-zero color count. The brand earns its restraint; every element is intentional, nothing is decorative.

--color-bone-cream.role: Primary surface — page background, menu pages, hero base
--color-bone-cream.context: warm bone-cream with the faintest age tint, like aged paper

--color-emerald-anchor.role: Primary text + ceremonial dark surfaces — menu covers, reservation chrome
--color-emerald-anchor.context: deep museum-green, the EMP signature color

--color-ink-body.role: Long-form body text on bone-cream — menu descriptions, philosophy text
--color-ink-body.context: warm off-black with a touch of green to harmonize with emerald-anchor

--color-gold-restraint.role: Editorial accent — section ornaments, hairline rules, special-occasion callouts
--color-gold-restraint.context: muted antique gold, used at most once per page as ceremonial punctuation

--color-shadow-surface.role: Alternating section background — chef-stories, ingredient sourcing
--color-shadow-surface.context: deeper-warm cream, the second-tone for slow rhythm

--color-fog-divider.context: cream-shadow divider, near-invisible — supports without showing

--color-graphite-body.role: Secondary body text — captions, dietary disclosures, footnotes
--color-graphite-body.context: warm grey calibrated for fine-print legibility on bone-cream

--font-display-cardo.role: Display headlines — hero, menu courses, philosophy titling

--font-body-grotesk.role: Body copy, navigation, reservation labels, dietary callouts

antiPatterns: Never use rounded corners on photography, hero, or chrome — EMP's design philosophy is square-edged restraint. Soft corners read as casual hospitality, not fine dining.
Never use --color-gold-restraint as a button fill or surface color — it appears at most once per page as ceremonial punctuation (a hairline, an ornament). As a theme it becomes 90s-banquet-hall gold.
Whitespace must be ceremonial — sections with content density above ~30% viewport coverage stop reading as EMP. The luxury IS the space.
Body type must never use --font-display-cardo — Cardo at body sizes looks like an academic journal. Serif lives at hero scale only; body is always grotesk.
Avoid drop-shadows entirely — EMP separates layers with whitespace and cream-tone steps. Any shadow undercuts the museum-restraint character.
